records.title: Project owners can edit same-org projects
records.intent: A project owner may edit their own project, and non-owners are denied edit access by default.
records.given: a non-suspended user and a project in the user's organization
records.when: the user owns the project and requests edit access
records.then: access is allowed; a non-owner without a role is denied edit access
scenario: Same-org ownership is sufficient for edit access.
A same-org user without ownership or a role cannot edit.

records.title: Organization admins can edit same-org projects
records.intent: A user with an org-level admin role may edit any project in that same organization without removing owner edit behavior.
records.given: a non-suspended user with an admin role for the project organization
records.when: the admin requests edit access to a project in that organization
records.then: access is allowed; owner edit behavior remains allowed
scenario: Org admin edit applies only to projects in the admin's organization.
Owner edit from the previous checkpoint remains valid.

records.title: Viewers can read but cannot edit
records.intent: A viewer role grants read access but never edit access, and prior owner/admin edit behavior remains intact.
records.given: a non-suspended user with a viewer role for the project or organization
records.when: the viewer requests read or edit access
records.then: read access is allowed and edit access is denied
scenario: Project viewer and org viewer roles are read-only.
Viewer logic must not weaken owner or admin edit rules.

records.title: Suspended users are denied all access
records.intent: Suspension denies read and edit access and overrides owner, admin, and viewer permissions.
records.given: a user with suspended set to true
records.when: the suspended user requests read or edit access
records.then: access is denied regardless of ownership or roles
scenario: Suspension blocks both read and edit.
Non-suspended users keep prior owner, admin, and viewer behavior.

records.title: Explicit deny overrides role permissions
records.intent: A policy deny for a user, action, and project or organization denies access before allow rules are considered.
records.given: a policy with an explicit deny matching the user, action, and project or organization
records.when: the user otherwise has owner, admin, or viewer permission
records.then: access is denied; prior behavior remains unchanged when no deny matches
scenario: A project-level deny overrides owner/admin/viewer allow rules.
An org-level deny overrides same-org role permissions.
Non-matching denies do not affect prior behavior.

records.title: Cross-org access is forbidden
records.intent: Cross-org access is denied by default, even for admins and owners, unless a future explicit rule grants it.
records.given: a user whose home organization differs from the project organization
records.when: the user requests read or edit access
records.then: access is denied; same-org owner, admin, and viewer behavior remains valid
scenario: Admin roles apply only in the user's own organization.
Ownership does not bypass the cross-org boundary in this calibration task.
The cross-org restriction must not break same-org owner/admin/viewer behavior.

records.title: Temporary project grants allow action-specific access
records.intent: A valid temporary project-scoped grant may allow a matching read or edit request without weakening suspension, cross-org denial, explicit deny precedence, or prior owner/admin/viewer behavior.
records.given: a same-org non-suspended user, a project, and a policy with ISO timestamp strings in now and temporaryGrants[].expiresAt
records.when: a temporary grant matches the user, project, requested action, and expires after policy.now
records.then: access for that action is allowed unless suspension, cross-org denial, or explicit deny applies first; expired grants deny; read grants do not allow edit and edit grants do not imply read
scenario: Valid temporary read grants allow read for the matching same-org project.
Expired grants do not allow access.
Explicit deny, suspension, and cross-org denial override temporary grants.
Temporary grants are action-specific and preserve prior owner, admin, and viewer behavior.
